title: Local-Global Attention Recovery
research_question: Once the local-attention path is debugged, do the remaining attention layers in the hybrid winner all need global context, or can some of them localize safely?

candidates.name: t1-upper-two-local-256
candidates.goal: Test whether the top of the remaining attention stack can localize safely.
candidates.hypothesis: The last two attention layers may not need full global context if the lower stack is already mixer-heavy.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether local attention can survive at the very top of the hybrid backbone.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the lightest valid local-global probe on the current winner and directly attacks an unresolved architecture question.
candidates.description: use a 256-token local window in the top two attention layers of the hybrid winner

candidates.name: t2-upper-four-local-256
candidates.goal: Test a broader local-global split on the hybrid backbone.
candidates.hypothesis: The whole upper attention stack may tolerate locality once the lower stack is already handled by mixers.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether local attention can replace most remaining global attention on the hybrid winner.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the natural next bracket after the lightest valid placement.
candidates.description: use a 256-token local window in four upper layers above the mixers

candidates.name: t3-top-two-local-512
candidates.goal: Test whether the earlier local-global failure was caused by an overly narrow local window.
candidates.hypothesis: A larger local window may preserve enough global structure to keep the hybrid winner intact.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether local attention is window-limited rather than conceptually wrong on this backbone.
candidates.why_worthy: This cleanly separates placement failure from window-size failure.
candidates.description: use a 512-token local window in the top two attention layers

candidates.name: t4-alternating-upper-local-256
candidates.goal: Interleave local and global attention instead of grouping them contiguously.
candidates.hypothesis: Alternating local and global attention may refresh global context often enough without paying for it in every attention block.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether alternating placement is cleaner than contiguous local bands.
candidates.why_worthy: This tests a distinct architecture theory, not just another window-size sweep.
candidates.description: alternate local attention through the remaining attention layers of the hybrid winner

candidates.name: t5-upper-two-local-128
candidates.goal: Push the safest local placement harder.
candidates.hypothesis: If top-local attention is viable, a smaller window may buy real savings before quality collapses.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the local-global idea has headroom once a safe placement is found.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the right aggressiveness test only after the safer top-two placement is represented in the tranche.
candidates.description: use a 128-token local window in the top two attention layers
